segments.text: He was wrong on both counts. And at least Szpindel had died knowing that Michelle cared for him.

runs.t: He was wrong on both counts. And at least Szpindel had died knowing that Michelle cared for him.

segments.text: Chelsea died thinking I just didn't give a shit.

runs.t: Chelsea died thinking I just didn't give a shit.

segments.text: It had been two years or more, and while we still interfaced occasionally we hadn't met in the flesh since the day she'd left. She came at me from right out of the Oort, sent an urgent voice message to my inlays: Cygnus. Please call NOW. It's important.

runs.t: It had been two years or more, and while we still interfaced occasionally we hadn't met in the flesh since the day she'd left. She came at me from right out of the Oort, sent an urgent voice message to my inlays:
Cygnus. Please call NOW. It's important.

segments.text: It was the first time since I'd known her that she'd ever blanked the optics.

runs.t: It was the first time since I'd known her that she'd ever blanked the optics.

segments.text: I knew it was important. I knew it was bad, even without picture. I knew because there was no picture, and I could tell it was worse than bad from the harmonics in her voice. I could tell it was lethal.

runs.t: I knew it was important. I knew it was bad, even without picture. I knew
because
there was no picture, and I could tell it was worse than bad from the harmonics in her voice. I could tell it was lethal.

segments.text: I found out afterwards that she'd gotten caught in the crossfire. The Realists had sown a fibrodysplasia variant outside the Boston catacombs; an easy tweak, a single-point retroviral whose results served both as an act of terrorism and an ironic commentary on the frozen paralysis of Heaven's occupants. It rewrote a regulatory gene controlling ossification on Chromosome 4, and rigged a metabolic bypass at three loci on 17.

runs.t: I found out afterwards that she'd gotten caught in the crossfire. The Realists had sown a fibrodysplasia variant outside the Boston catacombs; an easy tweak, a single-point retroviral whose results served both as an act of terrorism and an ironic commentary on the frozen paralysis of Heaven's occupants. It rewrote a regulatory gene controlling ossification on Chromosome 4, and rigged a metabolic bypass at three loci on 17.

segments.text: Chelsea started growing a new skeleton. Her joints were calcifying within fifteen hours of exposure, her ligaments and tendons within twenty. By then they were starving her at the cellular level, trying to slow the bug by depriving it of metabolites, but they could only buy time and not much of it. Twenty-three hours in, her striated muscles were turning to stone.

runs.t: Chelsea started growing a new skeleton. Her joints were calcifying within fifteen hours of exposure, her ligaments and tendons within twenty. By then they were starving her at the cellular level, trying to slow the bug by depriving it of metabolites, but they could only buy time and not much of it. Twenty-three hours in, her striated muscles were turning to stone.

segments.text: I didn't find this out immediately, because I didn't call her back. I didn't need to know the details. I could tell from her voice that she was dying. Obviously she wanted to say goodbye.

runs.t: I didn't find this out immediately, because I didn't call her back. I didn't need to know the details. I could tell from her voice that she was dying. Obviously she wanted to say goodbye.

segments.text: I couldn't talk to her until I knew how to do that.

runs.t: I couldn't talk to her until I knew how to do that.

segments.text: I spent hours scouring the noosphere, looking for precedents. There's no shortage of ways to die; I found millions of case records dealing with the etiquette. Last words, last vows, instruction manuals for the soon-to-bereaved. Palliative neuropharm. Extended and expository death scenes in popular fiction. I went through it all, assigned a dozen front-line filters to separate heat from light.

runs.t: I spent hours scouring the noosphere, looking for precedents. There's no shortage of ways to die; I found millions of case records dealing with the etiquette. Last words, last vows, instruction manuals for the soon-to-bereaved. Palliative neuropharm. Extended and expository death scenes in popular fiction. I went through it all, assigned a dozen front-line filters to separate heat from light.

segments.text: By the time she called again the news was out: acute Golem outbreak lancing like a white-hot needle through the heart of Boston. Containment measures holding. Heaven secure. Modest casualties expected. Names of victims withheld pending notification of kin.

runs.t: By the time she called again the news was out: acute Golem outbreak lancing like a white-hot needle through the heart of Boston. Containment measures holding. Heaven secure. Modest casualties expected. Names of victims withheld pending notification of kin.

segments.text: I still didn't know the principles, the rules: all I had were examples. Last wills and testaments; the negotiation of jumpers with their would-be rescuers; diaries recovered from imploded submarines or lunar crash sites. Recorded memoirs and deathbed confessions rattling into flatline. Black box transcripts of doomed spaceships and falling beanstalks, ending in fire and static. All of it relevant. None of it useful; none of it her.

runs.t: I still didn't know the principles, the
rules

segments.text: She called again, and still the optics were blank, and still I didn't answer.

runs.t: She called again, and still the optics were blank, and still I didn't answer.

segments.text: But the last time she called, she didn't spare me the view.

runs.t: But the last time she called, she didn't spare me the view.

segments.text: They'd made her as comfortable as possible. The gelpad conformed to every twisted limb, every erupting spur of bone. They would not have left her in any pain.

runs.t: They'd made her as comfortable as possible. The gelpad conformed to every twisted limb, every erupting spur of bone. They would not have left her in any pain.

segments.text: Her neck had torqued down and to the side as it petrified, left her staring at the twisted claw that had once been her right hand. Her knuckles were the size of walnuts. Plates and ribbons of ectopic bone distended the skin of her arms and shoulders, buried her ribs in a fibrous mat of calcified flesh.

runs.t: Her neck had torqued down and to the side as it petrified, left her staring at the twisted claw that had once been her right hand. Her knuckles were the size of walnuts. Plates and ribbons of ectopic bone distended the skin of her arms and shoulders, buried her ribs in a fibrous mat of calcified flesh.

segments.text: Movement was its own worst enemy. Golem punished even the slightest twitch, provoked the growth of fresh bone along any joints and surfaces conspiring to motion. Each hinge and socket had its own nonrenewable ration of flexibility, carved in stone; every movement depleted the account. The body seized incrementally. By the time she let me look at her, Chelsea had almost exhausted her degrees of freedom.

runs.t: Movement was its own worst enemy. Golem punished even the slightest twitch, provoked the growth of fresh bone along any joints and surfaces conspiring to motion. Each hinge and socket had its own nonrenewable ration of flexibility, carved in stone; every movement depleted the account. The body seized incrementally. By the time she let me look at her, Chelsea had almost exhausted her degrees of freedom.

segments.text: "Cyg," she slurred. "Know you're there."

runs.t: "Cyg," she slurred. "Know you're there."

segments.text: Her jaw was locked half-open; her tongue must have stiffened with every word. She did not look at the camera. She could not look at the camera.

runs.t: Her jaw was locked half-open; her tongue must have stiffened with every word. She did not look at the camera. She could not look at the camera.

segments.text: "Guess I know why you're not answ'ring. I'll try'nt—try not to take it pers'n'lly."

runs.t: "Guess I know why you're not answ'ring. I'll try'nt—
try not
to take it pers'n'lly."

segments.text: Ten thousand deathbed goodbyes arrayed around me, a million more within reach. What was I supposed to do, pick one at random? Stitch them into some kind of composite? All these words had been for other people. Grafting them onto Chelsea would reduce them to clichés, to trite platitudes. To insults.

runs.t: Ten thousand deathbed goodbyes arrayed around me, a million more within reach. What was I supposed to do, pick one at random? Stitch them into some kind of composite? All these words had been for other people. Grafting them onto Chelsea would reduce them to clichés, to trite platitudes. To insults.

segments.text: "Want t'say, don' feel bad. I know y're just— 's'not your fault, I guess. You'd pick up if you could."

runs.t: "Want t'say, don' feel bad. I know y're just— 's'not your fault, I guess. You'd pick up if you could."

segments.text: And say what? What do you say to someone who's dying in fast-forward before your eyes?

runs.t: And say what? What do you say to someone who's dying in fast-forward before your eyes?

segments.text: "Just keep trying t'connect, y'know. Can't help m'self…"

runs.t: "Just keep trying t'connect, y'know. Can't help m'self…"

segments.text: Although the essentials of this farewell are accurate, details from several deaths have been combined for dramatic purposes.

runs.t: Although the essentials of this farewell are accurate, details from several deaths have been combined for dramatic purposes

segments.text: "Please? Jus'—talk to me, Cyg…"

runs.t: "Please? Jus'—talk to me, Cyg…"

segments.text: More than anything, I wanted to.

runs.t: More than anything, I wanted to.

segments.text: "Siri, I…just…"

runs.t: "Siri, I…just…"

segments.text: I'd spent all this time trying to figure out how.

runs.t: I'd spent all this time trying to figure out
how

segments.text: "Forget't," she said, and disconnected.

runs.t: "Forget't," she said, and disconnected.

segments.text: I whispered something into the dead air. I don't even remember what.

runs.t: I whispered something into the dead air. I don't even remember what.

segments.text: I really wanted to talk to her.

runs.t: I really wanted to talk to her.

segments.text: I just couldn't find an algorithm that fit.

runs.t: I just couldn't find an algorithm that fit.

segments.text: "Ye shall know the truth, and the truth shall make you mad."

runs.t: "Ye shall know the truth, and the truth shall make you mad."

segments.text: —Aldous Huxley

runs.t: —Aldous Huxley

segments.text: They'd hoped, by now, to have banished sleep forever.

runs.t: They'd hoped, by now, to have banished sleep forever.

segments.text: The waste was nothing short of obscene: a third of every Human life spent with its strings cut, insensate, the body burning fuel but not producing. Think of all we could accomplish if we didn't have to lapse into unconsciousness every fifteen hours or so, if our minds could stay awake and alert from the moment of infancy to that final curtain call a hundred twenty years later. Think of eight billion souls with no off switch and no down time until the very chassis wore out.

runs.t: The waste was nothing short of obscene: a third of every Human life spent with its strings cut, insensate, the body burning fuel but not
producing

. Think of all we could accomplish if we didn't have to lapse into unconsciousness every fifteen hours or so, if our minds could stay awake and alert from the moment of infancy to that final curtain call a hundred twenty years later. Think of eight billion souls with no off switch and no down time until the very chassis wore out.

segments.text: Why, we could go to the stars.

runs.t: Why, we could go to the stars.

segments.text: It hadn't worked out that way. Even if we'd outgrown the need to stay quiet and hidden during the dark hours—the only predators left were those we'd brought back ourselves—the brain still needed time apart from the world outside. Experiences had to be catalogued and filed, mid-term memories promoted to long-term ones, free radicals swept from their hiding places among the dendrites. We had only reduced the need for sleep, not eliminated it—and that incompressible residue of downtime seemed barely able to contain the dreams and phantoms left behind. They squirmed in my head like creatures in a draining tidal pool.

runs.t: It hadn't worked out that way. Experiences had to be catalogued and filed, mid-term memories promoted to long-term ones, free radicals swept from their hiding places among the dendrites. We had only reduced the need for sleep, not eliminated it—and that incompressible residue of downtime seemed barely able to contain the dreams and phantoms left behind. segments.text: I woke.

runs.t: I woke.

segments.text: I was alone, weightless, in the center of my tent. I could have sworn something had tapped me on the back. Leftover hallucination, I thought. A lingering aftereffect of the haunted mansion, going for one last bit of gooseflesh en route to extinction.

segments.text: But it happened again. I bumped against the keelward curve of the bubble, bumped again, head and shoulder-blades against fabric; the rest of me came after, moving gently but irresistibly—

segments.text: No. Wrong direction. Theseus was rolling, like a harpooned whale at the surface of the sea. Turning her belly to the stars.

segments.text: I brought up ConSensus and threw a Nav-tac summary against the wall. A luminous point erupted from the outline of our ship, crawled away from Big Ben leaving a bright filament etched in its wake. I watched until the numbers read 15G.

segments.text: "Siri. My quarters, please."

runs.t: "Siri. My quarters, please."

segments.text: I jumped. It sounded as though the vampire had been at my very shoulder.

runs.t: I jumped. It sounded as though the vampire had been at my very shoulder.

segments.text: "Coming."

runs.t: "Coming."

segments.text: An ampsat relay, climbing at long last to an intercept with the Icarus antimatter stream. Somewhere behind the call of duty, my heart sank.

segments.text: We weren't running, Robert Cunningham's fondest wishes notwithstanding. Theseus was stockpiling ordnance.
